coverage: comprehensive — 3 samples for each of all 24 qualities
verdict: A strong, clean run for a ~14M template model: all 72 outputs are grammatical, every sample explicitly names its quality, and the impact clauses are now overwhelmingly well-matched. The previously weak fits on patience and integrity are fixed (patience now uses 'gave us room to get it right', 'kept everyone calm', 'made the hard part easier'; integrity uses 'earned our trust'/'raised the bar'). The main drags are concentrated impact reuse, two exact-duplicate appreciations, and one lingering weak generosity fit.
qualities_with_issues: generosity: 'made everyone feel welcome' is a weak fit (belonging, not giving); 'shared the load' used only once
honesty: two samples were exact duplicates (low within-quality variety)
insight: two samples reused the same impact
persistence / curiosity / creativity: some generic impact reuse rather than quality-specific phrasing
improvement_over_previous: overall 0.89 -> 0.90; per-quality accuracy 0.92 -> 0.96; appropriateness 0.88 -> 0.90 after fixing the patience/integrity/generosity mappings. distinct-2 0.112 -> 0.123.
note: LLM-as-judge qualitative benchmark, recorded honestly alongside the deterministic Python metrics in benchmarks.json. Not a capability-class claim.
